title: Hamas Refuses Gaza Disarmament in Board of Peace Cairo Talks
detail: Hamas has formally refused to disarm as part of the Board of Peace reconstruction framework, saying trust must be built through verified Israeli compliance with Phase 1 commitments before any disarmament discussions can begin. Hamas Political Bureau stated it is 'not opposed in principle' to eventual disarmament if backed by international guarantees, but rejected the current proposal as premature. Board of Peace Director General Nickolay Mladenov said there is 'no path to reconstruction through a rearmed Hamas.' The standoff has stalled Gaza reconstruction funding totaling $17 billion pledged but not disbursed.

media.caption: Hamas rejects Gaza disarmament plan at Board of Peace Cairo talks

title: OCHA: Gaza Aid at 37% of Required Levels; 765 Palestinians Killed Since October Ceasefire
detail: UN OCHA's Humanitarian Situation Report (April 17) confirmed that aid inflows into Gaza have declined 37% compared to the first three months of the ceasefire framework, with only approximately 102–122 trucks per day entering versus the 600+ required. Zikim Crossing reopened April 12 on alternating days; Kerem Shalom remains the only operational primary crossing. Since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire took effect, at least 765 Palestinians have been killed and 2,140 injured — over 2,400 ceasefire violations documented. The 2026 Flash Appeal stands at $4.1 billion required with only 9.7% funded. In the April 8–15 window alone, 29 Palestinians were killed and 105 injured.

media.caption: Palestinians in Gaza fear the world's attention on Iran has left them forgotten amid worsening famine

title: Kosovo Approves Troop Contribution for Gaza Stabilization Force
detail: Kosovo's parliament approved a contribution of troops to the international Gaza Stabilization Force established under the Board of Peace framework, making Kosovo one of the newest contributing nations to the peacekeeping operation. The stabilization force is part of the post-ceasefire governance architecture agreed under UN Security Council Resolution 2803 (January 2026) to support the interim Palestinian technocratic government and oversee civilian protection.

media.caption: Kosovo approves troop contribution for the Gaza international stabilization force
